factors.summary: Demand is accelerating due to new business and higher volumes.

evidence.text: Operating revenue increased 5% in both the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2015, driven by new business, higher volumes, and pricing increases.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating due to new business and higher volumes, indicating growth.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: Operating revenue growth in the third quarter of 2015 was due to new business and higher volumes, primarily in CPG/retail and technology/healthcare, and pricing increases.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company successfully raised prices, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Operating revenue (revenue excluding subcontracted transportation and fuel costs) increased 5% in both the third quarter and the nine months ended September 30, 2015.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, showing strong performance.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by real demand.

evidence.text: Operating revenue growth in the third quarter of 2015 was due to new business and higher volumes, primarily in CPG/retail and technology/healthcare, and pricing increases.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, driven by real demand, indicating positive trends.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly from foreign exchange and fuel costs.

evidence.text: Total revenue decreased 1% in both the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2015, which primarily reflects negative impacts from foreign exchange and lower fuel costs passed through to customers.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising, particularly from foreign exchange and fuel costs, which can negatively impact margins.

factors.summary: Margins are improving due to revenue growth and cost management.

evidence.text: SCS EBT increased 9% in the third quarter of 2015 and increased 27% in the nine months ended September 30, 2015 due to increased operating revenue.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Margins are improving due to revenue growth and cost management, showing strong financial health.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are stable with some foreign exchange headwinds.

evidence.text: The overall economic environment is stable, with foreign exchange impacts affecting revenue.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions are stable but face foreign exchange headwinds, resulting in mixed signals.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are acting as a headwind.

evidence.text: Negative impacts from foreign exchange and lower fuel costs passed through to customers, substantially offset by operating revenue growth.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements are acting as a headwind, negatively impacting revenue.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by increased capital expenditures.

evidence.text: We continue to expect full-year 2015 accrual basis capital expenditures from continuing operations to be $2.63 billion.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased capital expenditures indicate confidence in future growth.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include foreign exchange impacts and economic conditions.

evidence.text: Negative impacts from foreign exchange and lower fuel costs passed through to customers, substantially offset by operating revenue growth.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange impacts and lower fuel costs pose risks, albeit partially offset.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange and fuel costs are key sensitivities.

evidence.text: Negative impacts from foreign exchange and lower fuel costs passed through to customers, substantially offset by operating revenue growth.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange and fuel costs are significant sensitivities affecting performance.

factors.summary: Capex is increasing, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We continue to expect full-year 2015 accrual basis capital expenditures from continuing operations to be $2.63 billion.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures signal confidence in growth opportunities.

evidence.text: Ryder’s debt to equity ratios were 279% and 260% as of September 30, 2015 and December 31, 2014 , respectively.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising debt to equity ratios indicate increasing financial leverage risk.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is negative, indicating cash outflow.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Negative free cash flow indicates cash outflow and liquidity concerns.
